text: "Hath rendered the pride of Jacob."... He hath punished Jacob for his pride; and therefore Ninive must not expect to escape. Or else, rendering the pride of Jacob means rewarding, that is, punishing Ninive for the pride they exercised against Jacob.
"Of his mighty men."... He speaks of the Chaldeans and Medes sent to destroy Ninive.—Stupefied: consopiti. That is, they drive on furiously like men intoxicated with wine.
"Stumble in their march."... By running hastily on.
